v.CNV: 撒母耳年老的时候，立了他的儿子作以色列人的士师。
v.ESV: When Samuel became old, he made his sons judges over Israel.
v.GAE: 사무엘이 늙으매 그의 아들들을 이스라엘 사사로 삼으니
v.KJV: And it came to pass, when Samuel was old, that he made his sons judges over Israel.
v.NASB: And it came about when Samuel was old that he appointed his sons judges over Israel.
v.NIV: When Samuel grew old, he appointed his sons as Israel’s leaders.
v.NRSV: When Samuel became old, he made his sons judges over Israel.
v.SAENEW: 사무엘은 늙자, 자기의 아들들을 이스라엘의 사사로 세웠다.
v.WLB: 사무엘은 나이가 들자 자기 아들들을 이스라엘의 사사로 세웠습니다.

v.CNV: 他的长子名叫约珥，次子名叫亚比亚，都在别是巴作士师。
v.ESV: The name of his firstborn son was Joel, and the name of his second, Abijah; they were judges in Beersheba.
v.GAE: 장자의 이름은 요엘이요 차자의 이름은 아비야라 그들이 브엘세바에서 사사가 되니라
v.KJV: Now the name of his firstborn was Joel; and the name of his second, Abiah: they were judges in Beer-sheba.
v.NASB: Now the name of his firstborn was Joel, and the name of his second, Abijah; they were judging in Beersheba.
v.NIV: The name of his firstborn was Joel and the name of his second was Abijah, and they served at Beersheba.
v.NRSV: The name of his firstborn son was Joel, and the name of his second, Abijah; they were judges in Beer-sheba.
v.SAENEW: 맏아들의 이름은 요엘이요, 둘째 아들의 이름은 아비야다. 그들은 브엘세바에서 사사로 일하였다.
v.WLB: 맏아들의 이름은 요엘이고 둘째 아들의 이름은 아비야입니다. 그들은 브엘세바에서 사사가 됐습니다.

v.CNV: 两个儿子都没有行撒母耳的道路，反而转去追求不义之财，收受贿赂，屈枉正直。
v.ESV: Yet his sons did not walk in his ways but turned aside after gain. They took bribes and perverted justice.
v.GAE: 그의 아들들이 자기 아버지의 행위를 따르지 아니하고 이익을 따라 뇌물을 받고 판결을 굽게 하니라
v.KJV: And his sons walked not in his ways, but turned aside after lucre, and took bribes, and perverted judgment.
v.NASB: His sons, however, did not walk in his ways, but turned aside after dishonest gain and took bribes and perverted justice.
v.NIV: But his sons did not follow his ways. They turned aside after dishonest gain and accepted bribes and perverted justice.
v.NRSV: Yet his sons did not follow in his ways, but turned aside after gain; they took bribes and perverted justice.
v.SAENEW: 그러나 그 아들들은 아버지의 길을 따라 살지 않고, 돈벌이에만 정신이 팔려, 뇌물을 받고서, 치우치게 재판을 하였다.
v.WLB: 그러나 사무엘의 아들들은 사무엘이 걸었던 길을 가지 않았습니다. 그들은 부당한 이득을 따라 뇌물을 받고 옳지 않은 판결을 내렸습니다.

v.CNV: 于是以色列所有的长老都集合起来，到拉玛去见撒母耳，
v.ESV: Then all the elders of Israel gathered together and came to Samuel at Ramah
v.GAE: 이스라엘 모든 장로가 모여 라마에 있는 사무엘에게 나아가서
v.KJV: Then all the elders of Israel gathered themselves together, and came to Samuel unto Ramah,
v.NASB: Then all the elders of Israel gathered together and came to Samuel at Ramah;
v.NIV: So all the elders of Israel gathered together and came to Samuel at Ramah.
v.NRSV: Then all the elders of Israel gathered together and came to Samuel at Ramah,
v.SAENEW: 그래서 이스라엘의 모든 장로가 모여서, 라마로 사무엘을 찾아갔다.
v.WLB: 그래서 모든 이스라엘 장로들은 라마에 있는 사무엘을 찾아갔습니다.

v.CNV: 对他说：“你老了，看哪，你的儿子不行你的道路，现在求你为我们立一个王治理我们，好像列国一样。”
v.ESV: and said to him, “Behold, you are old and your sons do not walk in your ways. Now appoint for us a king to judge us like all the nations.”
v.GAE: 그에게 이르되 보소서 당신은 늙고 당신의 아들들은 당신의 행위를 따르지 아니하니 모든 나라와 같이 우리에게 왕을 세워 우리를 다스리게 하소서 한지라
v.KJV: And said unto him, Behold, thou art old, and thy sons walk not in thy ways: now make us a king to judge us like all the nations.
v.NASB: and they said to him, 'Behold, you have grown old, and your sons do not walk in your ways. Now appoint a king for us to judge us like all the nations.'
v.NIV: They said to him, “You are old, and your sons do not follow your ways; now appoint a king to lead us, such as all the other nations have.”
v.NRSV: and said to him, “You are old and your sons do not follow in your ways; appoint for us, then, a king to govern us, like other nations.”
v.SAENEW: 그들이 사무엘에게 말하였다. "보십시오, 어른께서는 늙으셨고, 아드님들은 어른께서 걸어오신 그 길을 따라 살지 않습니다. 그러므로 이제 모든 이방 나라들처럼, 우리에게 왕을 세워 주셔서, 왕이 우리를 다스리게 하여 주십시오."
v.WLB: 그들이 사무엘에게 말했습니다. “이제 당신은 늙었고 당신 아들들은 당신이 행한 길을 가고 있지 않습니다. 그러니 우리에게 왕을 세워 주셔서 다른 모든 나라처럼 왕이 우리를 다스리게 해 주십시오.”

v.CNV: 他们说“求你为我们立一个王治理我们”这件事，使撒母耳很不高兴，他就祷告耶和华。
v.ESV: But the thing displeased Samuel when they said, “Give us a king to judge us.” And Samuel prayed to the Lord .
v.GAE: 우리에게 왕을 주어 우리를 다스리게 하라 했을 때에 사무엘이 그것을 기뻐하지 아니하여 여호와께 기도하매
v.KJV: But the thing displeased Samuel, when they said, Give us a king to judge us. And Samuel prayed unto the LORD.
v.NASB: But the thing was displeasing in the sight of Samuel when they said, 'Give us a king to judge us.' And Samuel prayed to the LORD.
v.NIV: But when they said, “Give us a king to lead us,” this displeased Samuel; so he prayed to the Lord .
v.NRSV: But the thing displeased Samuel when they said, “Give us a king to govern us.” Samuel prayed to the LORD,
v.SAENEW: 그러나 사무엘은 왕을 세워 다스리게 해 달라는 장로들의 말에 마음이 상하여, 주님께 기도를 드렸더니,
v.WLB: 왕을 세워 다스리게 해 달라는 말을 들은 사무엘이 마음이 언짢아 여호와께 기도를 드렸습니다.

v.CNV: 耶和华对撒母耳说：“众民对你所说的一切，你只管听从，因为他们不是厌弃你，而是厌弃我，不要我作他们的王。
v.ESV: And the Lord said to Samuel, “Obey the voice of the people in all that they say to you, for they have not rejected you, but they have rejected me from being king over them.
v.GAE: 여호와께서 사무엘에게 이르시되 백성이 네게 한 말을 다 들으라 이는 그들이 너를 버림이 아니요 나를 버려 자기들의 왕이 되지 못하게 함이니라
v.KJV: And the LORD said unto Samuel, Hearken unto the voice of the people in all that they say unto thee: for they have not rejected thee, but they have rejected me, that I should not reign over them.
v.NASB: The LORD said to Samuel, 'Listen to the voice of the people in regard to all that they say to you, for they have not rejected you, but they have rejected Me from being king over them.
v.NIV: And the Lord told him: “Listen to all that the people are saying to you; it is not you they have rejected, but they have rejected me as their king.
v.NRSV: and the LORD said to Samuel, “Listen to the voice of the people in all that they say to you; for they have not rejected you, but they have rejected me from being king over them.
v.SAENEW: 주님께서 사무엘에게 말씀하셨다. "백성이 너에게 한 말을 다 들어 주어라. 그들이 너를 버린 것이 아니라, 나를 버려서 자기들의 왕이 되지 못하게 한 것이다.
v.WLB: 그러자 여호와께서 그에게 말씀하셨습니다. “백성들이 네게 하는 말을 다 들어주어라. 그들이 너를 버린 것이 아니라 나를 버려 내가 그들의 왕인 것을 거부하는 것이다.

v.CNV: 自从我把他们从埃及领上来的那天起，直到现在，他们怎样离开我，事奉别的神，他们对我所行的一切，也照样对你行。
v.ESV: According to all the deeds that they have done, from the day I brought them up out of Egypt even to this day, forsaking me and serving other gods, so they are also doing to you.
v.GAE: 내가 그들을 애굽에서 인도하여 낸 날부터 오늘까지 그들이 모든 행사로 나를 버리고 다른 신들을 섬김 같이 네게도 그리하는도다
v.KJV: According to all the works which they have done since the day that I brought them up out of Egypt even unto this day, wherewith they have forsaken me, and served other gods, so do they also unto thee.
v.NASB: 'Like all the deeds which they have done since the day that I brought them up from Egypt even to this day-- in that they have forsaken Me and served other gods-- so they are doing to you also.
v.NIV: As they have done from the day I brought them up out of Egypt until this day, forsaking me and serving other gods, so they are doing to you.
v.NRSV: Just as they have done to me, from the day I brought them up out of Egypt to this day, forsaking me and serving other gods, so also they are doing to you.
v.SAENEW: 그들은 내가 이집트에서 데리고 올라온 날부터 오늘까지, 하는 일마다 그렇게 하여, 나를 버리고 다른 신들을 섬기더니, 너에게도 그렇게 하고 있다.
v.WLB: 내가 그들을 이집트에서 이끌어 낸 때부터 오늘에 이르기까지 그들은 그렇게 해왔다. 그들이 나를 제쳐 두고 다른 신들을 섬긴 것처럼 네게도 그러는 것이다.

v.CNV: 现在你只管听从他们的话，但必须郑重警告他们，告诉他们那要统治他们的王要怎样治理他们。”
v.ESV: Now then, obey their voice; only you shall solemnly warn them and show them the ways of the king who shall reign over them.”
v.GAE: 그러므로 그들의 말을 듣되 너는 그들에게 엄히 경고하고 그들을 다스릴 왕의 제도를 가르치라
v.KJV: Now therefore hearken unto their voice: howbeit yet protest solemnly unto them, and shew them the manner of the king that shall reign over them.
v.NASB: 'Now then, listen to their voice; however, you shall solemnly warn them and tell them of the procedure of the king who will reign over them.'
v.NIV: Now listen to them; but warn them solemnly and let them know what the king who will reign over them will claim as his rights.”
v.NRSV: Now then, listen to their voice; only — you shall solemnly warn them, and show them the ways of the king who shall reign over them.”
v.SAENEW: 그러니 너는 이제 그들의 말을 들어 주되, 엄히 경고하여, 그들을 다스릴 왕의 권한이 어떠한 것인지를 알려 주어라."
v.WLB: 그러니 그들의 말을 들어주어라. 그러나 그들에게 엄중하게 경고해 왕이 어떤 권한이 있는지 알게 해 주어라.”

v.CNV: 于是，撒母耳把耶和华所讲的一切话都告诉那些向他要求一个王的民众，
v.ESV: So Samuel told all the words of the Lord to the people who were asking for a king from him.
v.GAE: 사무엘이 왕을 요구하는 백성에게 여호와의 모든 말씀을 말하여
v.KJV: And Samuel told all the words of the LORD unto the people that asked of him a king.
v.NASB: So Samuel spoke all the words of the LORD to the people who had asked of him a king.
v.NIV: Samuel told all the words of the Lord to the people who were asking him for a king.
v.NRSV: So Samuel reported all the words of the LORD to the people who were asking him for a king.
v.SAENEW: 사무엘은 왕을 세워 달라고 요구하는 백성들에게, 주님께서 하신 모든 말씀을 그대로 전하였다.
v.WLB: 사무엘은 왕을 세워 달라고 요구하는 백성들에게 여호와께서 하신 모든 말씀을 전해 주었습니다.

v.CNV: 说：“那要统治你们的王将这样治理你们：他必征用你们的儿子，派他们作他的战车兵、骑兵、在车前奔走的前锋，
v.ESV: He said, “These will be the ways of the king who will reign over you: he will take your sons and appoint them to his chariots and to be his horsemen and to run before his chariots.
v.GAE: 이르되 너희를 다스릴 왕의 제도는 이러하니라 그가 너희 아들들을 데려다가 그의 병거와 말을 어거하게 하리니 그들이 그 병거 앞에서 달릴 것이며
v.KJV: And he said, This will be the manner of the king that shall reign over you: He will take your sons, and appoint them for himself, for his chariots, and to be his horsemen; and some shall run before his chariots.
v.NASB: He said, 'This will be the procedure of the king who will reign over you: he will take your sons and place them for himself in his chariots and among his horsemen and they will run before his chariots.
v.NIV: He said, “This is what the king who will reign over you will claim as his rights: He will take your sons and make them serve with his chariots and horses, and they will run in front of his chariots.
v.NRSV: He said, “These will be the ways of the king who will reign over you: he will take your sons and appoint them to his chariots and to be his horsemen, and to run before his chariots;
v.SAENEW: "당신들을 다스릴 왕의 권한은 이러합니다. 그는 당신들의 아들들을 데려다가 그의 병거와 말을 다루는 일을 시키고, 병거 앞에서 달리게 할 것입니다.
v.WLB: “너희를 다스릴 왕은 이렇게 할 것이다. 그는 너희 아들들을 데려다가 자기 마차와 말을 돌보는 일을 시키고 그들을 마차 앞에서 달리게 할 것이다.

v.CNV: 又派他们作千夫长、五十夫长，替他耕田、收他的庄稼、替他制造作战的武器和战车上的装备。
v.ESV: And he will appoint for himself commanders of thousands and commanders of fifties, and some to plow his ground and to reap his harvest, and to make his implements of war and the equipment of his chariots.
v.GAE: 그가 또 너희의 아들들을 천부장과 오십부장을 삼을 것이며 자기 밭을 갈게 하고 자기 추수를 하게 할 것이며 자기 무기와 병거의 장비도 만들게 할 것이며
v.KJV: And he will appoint him captains over thousands, and captains over fifties; and will set them to ear his ground, and to reap his harvest, and to make his instruments of war, and instruments of his chariots.
v.NASB: 'He will appoint for himself commanders of thousands and of fifties, and some to do his plowing and to reap his harvest and to make his weapons of war and equipment for his chariots.
v.NIV: Some he will assign to be commanders of thousands and commanders of fifties, and others to plow his ground and reap his harvest, and still others to make weapons of war and equipment for his chariots.
v.NRSV: and he will appoint for himself commanders of thousands and commanders of fifties, and some to plow his ground and to reap his harvest, and to make his implements of war and the equipment of his chariots.
v.SAENEW: 그는 당신들의 아들들을 천부장과 오십부장으로 임명하기도 하고, 왕의 밭을 갈게도 하고, 곡식을 거두어들이게도 하고, 무기와 병거의 장비도 만들게 할 것입니다.
v.WLB: 그가 너희 아들들을 천부장과 오십부장으로 세울 것이며 자신을 위해 밭을 갈고 추수하게 할 것이고 전쟁을 위한 무기와 마차의 장비를 만들게 할 것이다.

v.CNV: 他必征用你们的女儿作配香膏的、烧饭的和烤饼的。
v.ESV: He will take your daughters to be perfumers and cooks and bakers.
v.GAE: 그가 또 너희의 딸들을 데려다가 향료 만드는 자와 요리하는 자와 떡 굽는 자로 삼을 것이며
v.KJV: And he will take your daughters to be confectionaries, and to be cooks, and to be bakers.
v.NASB: 'He will also take your daughters for perfumers and cooks and bakers.
v.NIV: He will take your daughters to be perfumers and cooks and bakers.
v.NRSV: He will take your daughters to be perfumers and cooks and bakers.
v.SAENEW: 그는 당신들의 딸들을 데려다가, 향유도 만들게 하고 요리도 시키고 빵도 굽게 할 것입니다.
v.WLB: 또 너희 딸들을 데려다가 향료 만드는 사람, 요리사, 빵 굽는 사람이 되게 할 것이다.

v.CNV: 他必夺去你们最好的田地、葡萄园和橄榄园，赐给自己的臣仆。
v.ESV: He will take the best of your fields and vineyards and olive orchards and give them to his servants.
v.GAE: 그가 또 너희의 밭과 포도원과 감람원에서 제일 좋은 것을 가져다가 자기의 신하들에게 줄 것이며
v.KJV: And he will take your fields, and your vineyards, and your oliveyards, even the best of them, and give them to his servants.
v.NASB: 'He will take the best of your fields and your vineyards and your olive groves and give them to his servants.
v.NIV: He will take the best of your fields and vineyards and olive groves and give them to his attendants.
v.NRSV: He will take the best of your fields and vineyards and olive orchards and give them to his courtiers.
v.SAENEW: 그는 당신들의 밭과 포도원과 올리브 밭에서 가장 좋은 것을 가져다가 왕의 신하들에게 줄 것이며,
v.WLB: 그는 너희 밭과 포도밭과 올리브 밭 가운데 제일 좋은 것을 골라 자기 신하들에게 줄 것이며

v.CNV: 你们撒种所得的和葡萄园所出的，他都征收十分之一，赐给他的太监和臣仆。
v.ESV: He will take the tenth of your grain and of your vineyards and give it to his officers and to his servants.
v.GAE: 그가 또 너희의 곡식과 포도원 소산의 십일조를 거두어 자기의 관리와 신하에게 줄 것이며
v.KJV: And he will take the tenth of your seed, and of your vineyards, and give to his officers, and to his servants.
v.NASB: 'He will take a tenth of your seed and of your vineyards and give to his officers and to his servants.
v.NIV: He will take a tenth of your grain and of your vintage and give it to his officials and attendants.
v.NRSV: He will take one-tenth of your grain and of your vineyards and give it to his officers and his courtiers.
v.SAENEW: 당신들이 둔 곡식과 포도에서도 열에 하나를 거두어 왕의 관리들과 신하들에게 줄 것입니다.
v.WLB: 너희 곡식과 포도 수확물의 10분의 1을 가져다가 자기의 관리와 신하들에게 줄 것이다.

v.CNV: 他又必征收你们的仆婢、最精壮的青年和驴，替他工作。
v.ESV: He will take your male servants and female servants and the best of your young men and your donkeys, and put them to his work.
v.GAE: 그가 또 너희의 노비와 가장 아름다운 소년과 나귀들을 끌어다가 자기 일을 시킬 것이며
v.KJV: And he will take your menservants, and your maidservants, and your goodliest young men, and your asses, and put them to his work.
v.NASB: 'He will also take your male servants and your female servants and your best young men and your donkeys and use them for his work.
v.NIV: Your male and female servants and the best of your cattle and donkeys he will take for his own use.
v.NRSV: He will take your male and female slaves, and the best of your cattle and donkeys, and put them to his work.
v.SAENEW: 그는 당신들의 남종들과 여종들과 가장 뛰어난 젊은이들과 나귀들을 끌어다가 왕의 일을 시킬 것입니다.
v.WLB: 너희 남종들과 여종들과 너희 소년들과 나귀들 가운데 가장 좋은 것을 끌어다가 자기 일을 시키고

v.CNV: 你们的羊群，他要征收十分之一；连你们自己也作他的奴仆。
v.ESV: He will take the tenth of your flocks, and you shall be his slaves.
v.GAE: 너희의 양 떼의 십분의 일을 거두어 가리니 너희가 그의 종이 될 것이라
v.KJV: He will take the tenth of your sheep: and ye shall be his servants.
v.NASB: 'He will take a tenth of your flocks, and you yourselves will become his servants.
v.NIV: He will take a tenth of your flocks, and you yourselves will become his slaves.
v.NRSV: He will take one-tenth of your flocks, and you shall be his slaves.
v.SAENEW: 그는 또 당신들의 양 떼 가운데서 열에 하나를 거두어 갈 것이며, 마침내 당신들까지 왕의 종이 될 것입니다.
v.WLB: 너희 양들의 10분의 1을 가질 것이며 결국 너희 자신도 그의 노예가 될 것이다.

v.CNV: 到时，你们必因你们为自己拣选的王而哀求，那时耶和华却不应允你们。”
v.ESV: And in that day you will cry out because of your king, whom you have chosen for yourselves, but the Lord will not answer you in that day.”
v.GAE: 그 날에 너희는 너희가 택한 왕으로 말미암아 부르짖되 그 날에 여호와께서 너희에게 응답하지 아니하시리라 하니
v.KJV: And ye shall cry out in that day because of your king which ye shall have chosen you; and the LORD will not hear you in that day.
v.NASB: 'Then you will cry out in that day because of your king whom you have chosen for yourselves, but the LORD will not answer you in that day.'
v.NIV: When that day comes, you will cry out for relief from the king you have chosen, but the Lord will not answer you in that day. ”
v.NRSV: And in that day you will cry out because of your king, whom you have chosen for yourselves; but the LORD will not answer you in that day.”
v.SAENEW: 그 때에야 당신들이 스스로 택한 왕 때문에 울부짖을 터이지만, 그 때에 주님께서는 당신들의 기도에 응답하지 않으실 것입니다."
v.WLB: 그때가 되면 너희는 너희가 선택한 그 왕에게서 벗어나게 해 달라고 울부짖겠지만 그때는 여호와께서 너희의 말에 응답하지 않으실 것이다.”

v.CNV: 众民竟不肯听从撒母耳的话，说：“不，我们要一个王治理我们，
v.ESV: But the people refused to obey the voice of Samuel. And they said, “No! But there shall be a king over us,
v.GAE: 백성이 사무엘의 말 듣기를 거절하여 이르되 아니로소이다 우리도 우리 왕이 있어야 하리니
v.KJV: Nevertheless the people refused to obey the voice of Samuel; and they said, Nay; but we will have a king over us;
v.NASB: Nevertheless, the people refused to listen to the voice of Samuel, and they said, 'No, but there shall be a king over us,
v.NIV: But the people refused to listen to Samuel. “No!” they said. “We want a king over us.
v.NRSV: But the people refused to listen to the voice of Samuel; they said, “No! but we are determined to have a king over us,
v.SAENEW: 이렇게 일러주어도 백성은, 사무엘의 말을 듣지 않고 말하였다. "그렇지 않습니다. 우리에게도 왕이 있어야 되겠습니다.
v.WLB: 이렇게 말했지만 백성들은 사무엘의 말을 들으려 하지 않았습니다. “아니오. 그래도 우리는 왕을 원합니다.

v.CNV: 让我们也像列国一样，有自己的王治理我们，帅领我们出征，为我们作战。”
v.ESV: that we also may be like all the nations, and that our king may judge us and go out before us and fight our battles.”
v.GAE: 우리도 다른 나라들 같이 되어 우리의 왕이 우리를 다스리며 우리 앞에 나가서 우리의 싸움을 싸워야 할 것이니이다 하는지라
v.KJV: That we also may be like all the nations; and that our king may judge us, and go out before us, and fight our battles.
v.NASB: that we also may be like all the nations, that our king may judge us and go out before us and fight our battles.'
v.NIV: Then we will be like all the other nations, with a king to lead us and to go out before us and fight our battles.”
v.NRSV: so that we also may be like other nations, and that our king may govern us and go out before us and fight our battles.”
v.SAENEW: 우리도 모든 이방 나라들처럼, 우리의 왕이 우리를 다스리며, 그 왕이 우리를 이끌고 나가서, 전쟁에서 싸워야 할 것입니다."
v.WLB: 다른 나라들처럼 우리를 다스릴 뿐 아니라 우리를 이끌고 나가 싸워 줄 왕이 있어야 합니다.”

v.CNV: 撒母耳听了众民的一切话，就向耶和华复述。
v.ESV: And when Samuel had heard all the words of the people, he repeated them in the ears of the Lord .
v.GAE: 사무엘이 백성의 말을 다 듣고 여호와께 아뢰매
v.KJV: And Samuel heard all the words of the people, and he rehearsed them in the ears of the LORD.
v.NASB: Now after Samuel had heard all the words of the people, he repeated them in the LORD'S hearing.
v.NIV: When Samuel heard all that the people said, he repeated it before the Lord .
v.NRSV: When Samuel had heard all the words of the people, he repeated them in the ears of the LORD.
v.SAENEW: 사무엘이 백성의 모든 말을 듣고 나서, 주님께서 들으시도록 다 아뢰니,
v.WLB: 사무엘이 백성들의 말을 모두 듣고 여호와께 다 말씀드렸더니

v.CNV: 耶和华对撒母耳说：“你只管听从他们的话，为他们立一个王。”于是撒母耳对以色列人说：“你们各人回自己的城去吧。”
v.ESV: And the Lord said to Samuel, “Obey their voice and make them a king.” Samuel then said to the men of Israel, “Go every man to his city.”
v.GAE: 여호와께서 사무엘에게 이르시되 그들의 말을 들어 왕을 세우라 하시니 사무엘이 이스라엘 사람들에게 이르되 너희는 각기 성읍으로 돌아가라 하니라
v.KJV: And the LORD said to Samuel, Hearken unto their voice, and make them a king. And Samuel said unto the men of Israel, Go ye every man unto his city.
v.NASB: The LORD said to Samuel, 'Listen to their voice and appoint them a king.' So Samuel said to the men of Israel, 'Go every man to his city.'
v.NIV: The Lord answered, “Listen to them and give them a king.” Then Samuel said to the Israelites, “Everyone go back to your own town.”
v.NRSV: The LORD said to Samuel, “Listen to their voice and set a king over them.” Samuel then said to the people of Israel, “Each of you return home.”
v.SAENEW: 주님께서 사무엘에게 말씀하셨다. "너는 그들의 말을 받아들여서 그들에게 왕을 세워 주어라." 그래서 사무엘은 이스라엘 사람들에게, 각자 자기의 성읍으로 돌아가라고 일렀다.
v.WLB: 여호와께서 대답하셨습니다. “그들의 말대로 그들에게 왕을 세워 주어라.” 그러자 사무엘은 이스라엘 사람들에게 모두 자기 성으로 돌아가 있으라고 말했습니다.
